public_api_changes.new_symbol: func NewOrchestrator(guard *FileGuard, cat *catalog.Catalog, w *wal.Writer, opts ...Option) (*Orchestrator, error)
public_api_changes.reason: variadic opts appended; all existing call sites (3-arg calls) compile unchanged

behavior_changes.change: BeginSplit no longer immediately returns ErrAlreadySplitting when guard.TryAcquire fails; it first checks reclaimIfExpired and retries TryAcquire once if a stale lease was reclaimed.
behavior_changes.mitigation: Only triggers when a previously-recorded lease (set exclusively by a prior successful BeginSplit on THIS Orchestrator instance) has expired per the injected/default clock. Verified via TestSplittingStatusIsolation (pre-existing) and the new TestAbandonedSplittingRecoversAfterTimeout that immediate re-BeginSplit (well within default 30s lease) still returns ErrAlreadySplitting.

behavior_changes.change: EndSplit now also clears the fileID's lease entry via a deferred clearLease call, regardless of outcome.
behavior_changes.mitigation: Purely additive bookkeeping cleanup; does not affect EndSplit's existing return values or guard-release behavior.

downstream_consumers_checked.finding: Uses Orchestrator.BeginSplit/EndSplit/AbortSplit via existing signatures only; not touched, not affected.
Only call sites are within engine/split's own test files; no other package constructs an Orchestrator directly.
